checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document follows the slide-by-slide layout of the original PDF presentation. Content blocks for titles, bulleted lists, and images are organized sequentially. The transition from a landscape slide deck to a vertical scrolling HTML page is handled correctly without disrupting the logical flow of information.

The table on slide 3 ('Road Type # %') is rendered correctly in the HTML with clear borders and alignment, making it easily readable. Other list-like structures (e.g., Slide 8, Slide 11) are also well-organized and maintain the visual integrity of the original information.

All meaningful images from the source PDF, including the complex timeline on slide 4, the various maps (slides 5, 9, 10), drone photos (slides 5, 6, 7), charts (slides 3, 10), and decorative logos (NCDIT logo appearing on most slides), are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content. Each slide's text and graphics appear exactly once in the main body of the document. The repetition of the NCDIT logo is faithful to the source PDF where it appears as a template element on almost every page.
